description: Fix game core logic: update clans to 6 with proper powers, implement correct mandate draw system (draw 3, pick 1, return 2), and fix Tea Ceremony alliance phase
steps: 1. Update src/types/game.ts - CLANS array: Replace the existing 5 clans with 6 clans themed for feudal Japan area control. The 6 clans should be: (1) Koi clan - power related to movement/migration of forces (can move to non-adjacent regions or move extra distance), (2) Dragonfly clan - power related to honor/victory by honor (gains extra VP from honor, starts with higher honor), (3) Bonsai clan - power related to recruitment/growth (recruits extra forces, starts with more reserves), (4) Fox clan - power related to deception/betrayal (can move through enemy regions, surprise mechanics), (5) Lotus clan - power related to mandates/politics (gets to draw extra mandate tiles, political advantage), (6) Turtle clan - power related to defense/resistance (loses fewer forces in battle, shrine bonuses). Each clan needs: id, name, color, ability name, abilityDescription. Keep the same Clan interface.

2. Update src/types/game.ts - Add 'drawnMandates' field to GameState interface: drawnMandates: MandateType[] (holds the 3 drawn mandates the active player is choosing from). Also add 'mandateChoicePhase' boolean field to track when player is choosing.

3. Update src/utils/gameLogic.ts - createInitialGameState: Update all references to old clan IDs to use new clan IDs. Update homeRegions array to include 6 entries. Fix initial honor/reserves based on new clan powers (Dragonfly starts higher honor, Bonsai starts more reserves).

4. Update src/utils/gameLogic.ts - Implement the mandate draw system: Create function drawThreeMandates(state) that draws 3 from mandatesDeck and puts them in state.drawnMandates. Create function chooseMandateFromDrawn(state, chosenMandate, playerId) that takes the chosen mandate, executes it, returns the other 2 to the TOP of mandatesDeck, and clears drawnMandates. The Lotus clan power should let them draw 4 instead of 3.

5. Update src/utils/gameLogic.ts - Update executeRecruit, resolveBattleBids, moveForces, scoreRegions: Replace old clan ID checks (iron-crane, shadow-fox, crimson-bear, storm-dragon, jade-turtle) with new clan IDs and their corresponding powers. Koi: can move to non-adjacent (2 hops), Dragonfly: +1 VP when scoring if honor is highest, Bonsai: recruit +1 extra, Fox: can move through enemy regions, Lotus: (handled in mandate draw), Turtle: loses 1 fewer force + shrine VP bonus.

6. Update src/utils/gameLogic.ts - Fix Tea Ceremony phase: The advancePhase function for 'tea' phase should properly handle the alliance proposal flow. Every player should get a turn to propose alliances during tea phase. Add a teaCeremonyComplete flag or track which players have had their tea turn. When all players have had their turn, advance to politics.

7. Update src/store/gameStore.ts - Replace issueMandate action with two new actions: drawMandates() that calls drawThreeMandates, and chooseMandateAction(mandate: MandateType) that calls chooseMandateFromDrawn. Update flow: when politics phase starts and it's a player's turn, they first drawMandates, then from the 3 shown they chooseMandateAction.

8. Update src/store/gameStore.ts - Fix tea ceremony actions: Ensure doProposeAlliance and doAcceptAlliance work correctly for all players in hotseat mode. Add doEndTeaTurn() action that advances to next player's tea turn or advances phase if all have gone.

9. Update src/components/ActionPanel.tsx - Tea phase: Show current player's alliance options. Show 'End My Tea Turn' button. Show pending proposals for the current player. Each player gets a turn to propose/accept.

10. Update src/components/ActionPanel.tsx - Politics phase: Instead of showing all 5 mandate buttons, show the 3 drawn mandates as buttons (from gameState.drawnMandates). If drawnMandates is empty, show a 'Draw Mandates' button. Player clicks one of the 3 to choose it.
11. Update src/components/MainMenu.tsx - Update player count selector to allow 2-6 players. Update default clan assignments to use new clan IDs.
12. Update src/components/PlayerPanel.tsx, BattlePanel.tsx, GameOverScreen.tsx - Update any references to old CLANS that may break with new clan IDs.
acceptance_criteria: TypeScript compilation (npx tsc -b) passes with no errors
CLANS array in game.ts contains exactly 6 clans with unique IDs, names, colors, and abilities matching the specified powers
During politics phase, player draws 3 mandates (4 for Lotus clan), picks 1, and the other 2 go back to top of deck
Tea Ceremony allows each player in turn to propose/accept alliances before advancing to politics
All clan power implementations reference the new clan IDs consistently
